name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Something strange is happening in Trump-land
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Trump's campaign is ramping up | Friend --

A strange thing has been happening to the Trump campaign over the last few weeks.

First, they raised $51 million dollars with the help of the RNC in June (which is dangerously close to **[outraising us](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjU5NzY1NDcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxOTQ3NTg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)**)! Don't forget that almost $4 million of that money came directly from a check that Trump wrote himself.

Then, they started to grow their campaign organization. They're beefing up their communications team, for instance -- literally hiring the old communications director from Citizens United (the organization that was the subject of that charming Supreme Court case) to run an operation deploying surrogates who will amplify Trump's message all over the country. There's now a rapid response director to come up with delightful, new attacks against Hillary. They even brought on a digital director -- presumably to ramp up their Twitter output.

Meanwhile, the Republican Party began nailing down its official platform with a few of Trump’s most extreme suggestions at the center -- like this wall we’ve heard so much about.

And sometime this week, we'll meet Trump's vice presidential candidate. We hear that the shortlist includes names like Newt Gingrich, Mike Pence, and Chris Christie. We'll have more to say about this crew later.

With every passing day, we're watching Trump's campaign become more professional. We're seeing him add more capacity for big-dollar fundraising, get more sophisticated with message coordination, and bring on all the other capabilities we expect from a modern presidential operation.

And by the way, all of that's happening while Trump's still Trump -- still able to dominate the attention of the media and get millions and millions of dollars in free airtime any time he says something outrageous.

So if you had yourself convinced that Trump's chances were hopeless, it's time to start thinking otherwise.

**Trump is a real competitor, and it’s going to take real resources to take him down. So chip in to get your free Team Hillary sticker and say you’re committed to stopping Trump for good:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjU5NzY1NDcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxOTQ3NTg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

Christina Reynolds
Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

name: Eric Hyers
subject: this just in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---

Friend,

Just in -- Greg Gianforte has now topped more than a million dollars in spending on TV ads. And it is not even the middle of July. **This is a record amount of money for a Montana governor 's race at this point. We need your help now. [Donate $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=9gt3AL2tC%2BLTeydBTJUl7pAP7q3Sv6TG)**

You've probably seen them -- it's nearly impossible to watch the evening news or your favorite show without being inundated with Mr. Gianforte's commercials. But it's what his ads don't mention that's important: budget-busting tax giveaways to millionaires like himself, support for job-killing discriminatory policies that have already costs states like Indiana and North Carolina tens of millions of dollars and the lawsuit he filed against Montana to block public stream access on his property.

Steve shares our Montana values and he's stood up for them time and time again. But right now he is getting massively outspent.

To date, Greg Gianforte has bought $1.2 million worth of TV advertising across Montana -- he's banking on being able to just buy his way into the governor's office. **We 're counting on you not to let him. [Pitch in $15 now.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=QZtCNBcsq4wXbGsemJen25AP7q3Sv6TG)**

Thanks,

Eric Hyers

name: Mike Weinholtz
subject: Respecting the right to choose
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Weinholtz for Governor
body:
| | Did you catch the big news this week?
---
| | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1780838299716089088/-1139744964003820800?am=&recipientId=ENzs9AKZ1wqBssNbEIT6L&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com) Did you catch the big news this week, Peter? On Tuesday, a federal judge sided with the Planned Parenthood Association of Utah, striking down Gov. Herbert's attempt to block their federal funding. The court ruled that Herbert's action was unconstitutional, saying that it was driven by his politics and meant to "punish" and "weaken the organization." The judge also pointed out that the anti-abortion videos that triggered the move have been proven false--which Herbert himself had acknowledged earlier. In Utah we respect and recognize the right of all people to believe whatever they choose. However, we don't recognize the right of any politician to waste Utah taxpayer dollars on unwinnable lawsuits just to pursue their own unconstitutional whims. **[Will you give $25 now to help us correct course this November?](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1780838299716089088/-1139744964003820800?am=25&recipientId=ENzs9AKZ1wqBssNbEIT6L&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)** In Utah we know that family comes first. That's why we should always do what's best for families, which means supporting healthcare providers like Planned Parenthood. The Planned Parenthood Association of Utah offers vital healthcare services like pregnancy testing, HIV screening, and more--for both men and women. As governor, I'll empower healthcare providers do their jobs, and use our hard-earned taxpayer dollars wisely to solve real problems. **[Give $25 today to help make change a reality in Utah!](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1780838299716089088/-1139744964003820800?am=25&recipientId=ENzs9AKZ1wqBssNbEIT6L&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)** Let's do this!
Mike | Paid for by Mike Weinholtz for Governor

name: Ben Carson
subject: The 120 day plan is underway
body:
Peter,

About 2 weeks ago, the team at My Faith Votes got together for an
important strategic planning session, and they put together an
extremely impressive 120-day plan to identify, educate, and
active Christian voters.

This robust plan will leverage key Christian influencers --
people like David Jeremiah, Josh McDowell, Kirk Carmeron, James
Robison and others -- and over the course of 120 days, hold over
45 events, reach 45 million people of faith, and mobilize 1.3
million nonvoters in swing states.

Just this past weekend, we held our first My Faith Votes LIVE
Event and over 15,000 people attended.

We are onto something big here, and we are now stepping on the
gas. Will you make a secure, tax-deductible donation to help us
hit our goals?

To empower our key influencers and mobilize the silent Christian
community, we must reach our $3.3 million budget goal. Even if
it's just $25, your support today will help to get us there.

I'm proud of the team here. They know what they want to achieve
and they've put a bold plan in place to get there.

How many times have you heard about the 25 million Christians who
did not vote in 2012? The fact is, their participation could have
meant a world of difference. I decided to become the Honorary
National Chairman of My Faith Votes because I'm tired of thinking
about what could have been. It's time for us to do something
about it. We can each do something.

I hope you will help with a secure, tax-deductible donation
today. We have it in our power to make a real difference in 2016,
and I thank you for your continued commitment.

Thank you, and God Bless you.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: Ending domestic violence (need your signature)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c24/738133e4/37ca5ab4/7f82a9f0/1080387840/VEsH/ "https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/e160713")
---
| Team,

The Supreme Court last month barred people convicted of domestic violence from buying guns. It was the right decision.

**In homes where domestic violence happens, a gun in the house increases the risk of homicide by 600 percent.** And in domestic disputes that end in a death, between 60% and 70% involve a firearm.

Guns and domestic abuse are a deadly combination, which is why this ruling is the epitome of common sense. It will save lives. Period.

But I believe there is still more we can do.

[_**Stand by the Supreme Court's decision and show your support for taking additional measures to protect Americans from domestic violence. Sign here.**_](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c24/738133e4/37ca5ab4/7f82a9f0/1080387840/VEsE/ "https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/e160713")

I am committed to taking meaningful steps to prevent domestic violence. I authored a measure that will waive document replacement fees for domestic violence survivors who flee their abusers, and I helped lead the fight to reauthorize the _Violence Against Women Act._

Of course, to stop horrific acts of domestic violence, we also need to protect victims from the weapons used most often in deadly instances -- guns. That's why I promise to continue to push for universal background checks and common-sense gun safety laws.

No one should have to face the threat of deadly violence in day-to-day life. [_**Stand with me and support common-sense measures to stop domestic violence.**_](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c24/738133e4/37ca5ab4/7f82a9f0/1080387840/VEsF/ "https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/e160713")

Thank you,

Patrick |

subject: My jobs plan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Yesterday, I announced my plan to create jobs across our state and to create a North Carolina that works for everyone.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120260d/2fde7c66/4005b510/156a73e1/2424978101/VEsH/ "http://www.roycooper.com/issues/jobs")
---
Friend, My parents taught me the value of hard work, and they put my brother and me to work on the farm at an early age. I learned an appreciation for the good job you could get if you studied hard, and the fair shake you deserved if you did your part. Now, North Carolina's middle class has been left behind. Small business owners -- the backbone of NC's economy -- continue to face a growing number of challenges. Our state should support economic opportunity for all. **[Yesterday, I announced my plan to create jobs across our state and to create a North Carolina that works for everyone. Read it here.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120260d/2fde7c66/4005b510/156a73e1/2424978101/VEsE/ "http://www.roycooper.com/issues/jobs")** Governor McCrory's plans for North Carolina are clear. In the dark of night, he put political ideology ahead of our best interests and signed one of the nation's most discriminatory laws. So far, it's cost us thousands of jobs and hundreds of millions of dollars. That is unacceptable. **[My plan will help us reinvest in our middle class and encourage the growth of small businesses. When our economy grows, your paycheck should grow as well.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120260d/2fde7c66/4005b510/156a73e1/2424978101/VEsF/ "http://www.roycooper.com/issues/jobs")** Thanks for joining our fight, Roy | | [**Read my plan ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120260d/2fde7c66/4005b510/156a73e1/2424978101/VEsC/ "http://www.roycooper.com/issues/jobs")

subject: I’ve been digging into the numbers, and here's where we are:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Trump is pulling together an operation | Friend --

I’ve been digging into the numbers and keeping an eye on where we’re at.

So here’s the lay of the land: Trump raised $51 million in June (after he raised only $3 million in May) -- a staggering improvement. The Republican convention is next week, which will mean another huge influx of cash for Trump as he formally accepts the nomination. He’s hiring new staff, and is days from picking his running mate. Their operation is pulling together faster and more efficiently than seemed possible just a few weeks ago.

It’s time for us to step up as well. We have the best team of supporters behind us, but Trump is gaining ground, and as of right now, we’re only 16% toward our July fundraising goal -- a number we have to hit in order to keep building our campaign in all 50 states. There's just no guarantee that we'll hit that goal.

We’re counting on you. Our budget and strategy depends on you stepping up over the next few weeks -- the sooner, the better.

**Chip in $10 or more to keep some distance between him and our campaign, and when you do, we’ll send you a free Love Trumps Hate button as a thank you:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjU5ODI3ODES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMDY3NDE2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Dennis

name: Chris Koster
subject: WATCH: When victims came into my office
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-2206035284497922048/-5716898013611948032?utm_campaign=fnd_justic_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160714_fnd_gq_justic_act__ck)

Hi,
In 1994, the people of Cass County elected me to serve as their Prosecuting Attorney. During my 10 years in Harrisonville, I supervised litigation in thousands of cases, including many of our state's most heinous crimes.

When victims came to our office, it didn't matter what their race, gender, background, or family life was -- I worked to deliver justice to everyone who had been wronged. **[In our latest video, I talk about how these experiences shaped my outlook on public service.](https://act.myngp.com/el/-2206035284497922048/-5716898013611948032?utm_campaign=fnd_justic_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160714_fnd_gq_justic_act__ck)**

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-2206035284497922048/-5716898013611948032?utm_campaign=fnd_justic_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160714_fnd_gq_justic_act__ck)

We can never put things back the way they were for a victim, but we can show them that criminals will be punished. I was honored to serve in this role for the people of Harrisonville, and as your Governor, I'll continue working to bring a sense of equality and justice to all Missourians.

Thank you,

Chris Koster

[DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/-2206035284497922048/-5716898013611948032?utm_campaign=fnd_justic_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160714_fnd_gq_justic_act__ck)

name: Bullock HQ
subject: trouble with the truth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---

Friend,

| [](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Mluax9Fb6ktAzwPY0kd6bzqQ50B5JveM)
---

It's becoming a pattern -- Greg Gianforte is having trouble shooting straight with Montanans. So much so that the _Great Falls Tribune_ just called him out, stating that "he and his staff need to do better research and fact checking." Ouch.

**[Help us hold him accountable. Chip in $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=DeH%2BLP1tHeuMo2%2BT7%2FqRQzqQ50B5JveM)**

Mr. Gianforte claimed that he talked to a Facebook executive about bringing a data center to Montana.
⇒**He didn't. **

He said he spoke with Sanders County commissioners who were upset about the state buying a ranch. Then he said it was Mineral County commissioners.
⇒**Commissioners in both counties say this in false.**

He repeatedly bragged about an endorsement from the Montana Wood Products Association.
⇒**They didn't endorse him.**

And, maybe most bizarrely, he keeps saying that he never filed suit against the state to block public stream access on his property.
⇒**He did. **

The thing is, if we don't have the resources to call him out, the truth is going to get lost in the shuffle. Greg Gianforte has spent more than $1.2 million on TV already -- shattering previous records.

So, we thought it made sense to hit the road and hear from Wisconsin seniors who benefit from Social Security, to see what they thought about Senator Johnson's comments. The question was simple: "Sen. Johnson calls Social Security a 'Ponzi scheme.' Do you think Social Security is a Ponzi scheme?" **[Their answers were priceless. Click here to see for yourself.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4384/1c469400/6081789/356df50/2862693339/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/russfeingold/videos/10153835579563215")** [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4384/1c469400/6081789/356df53/2862693339/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/russfeingold/videos/10153835579563215/") My personal favorite: "I think Senator Johnson has no idea what he's talking about!" Senator Johnson's been working hard to undermine Social Security. He supported a plan privatize the program, which could be devastating for seniors across America. For many, Social Security is just helping them get by, and taking a risk with this program jeopardizes their futures. Unlike Senator Johnson, Russ has been traveling across Wisconsin to meet with seniors and listen to their concerns. They're worried about the increasing cost of prescription medications, and what might happen to their Social Security benefits if Senator Johnson were re-elected. **[All seniors should be able to retire with dignity. Take a minute to watch our conversations with Wisconsin seniors on Facebook, then share it with your friends and family.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4384/1c469400/6081789/356df50/2862693339/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/russfeingold/videos/10153835579563215")** Thanks, Erin Wagner

**Phil Scott Reacts to Administration's Decision on EB-5 Investor Solicitation**
Montpelier, Vt. – Lt. Governor Phil Scott today issued the following statement on recent revelations the Shumlin Administration allowed Jay Peak’s owners to continue to solicit investors months after the federal Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) launched its investigation:

“As a public servant and candidate for Governor, my priorities are growing the economy, making Vermont more affordable and restoring faith and trust in state government. With proper oversight, the EB-5 program can be a useful tool for stimulating much-needed investment and economic growth in Vermont.

“I was disappointed to learn from [Vermont Public Radio](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7f7e0d508f&e=19cebfe173)’s recent report that the Shumlin Administration enabled the owners of the EB-5 projects in the Northeast Kingdom, who are accused of multiple counts of fraud, to continue to solicit investors for months after the SEC had suspended that permission for Jay Peak. Even after repeatedly failing to provide compliance information to state regulators, they were allowed to raise more money. By the Administration’s own admission, it was a ‘calculated risk.’ Yet, they've not yet explained why they took this risk or why they allowed the problem to continue to grow.

“Whatever their reasons for allowing the scheme to continue while under federal investigation, the fact that journalists had to dig up this information further undermines faith and trust in state government.

“It also points to the lack of accountability and checks and balances that develop in a system of government controlled entirely by one party.

“In order to grow the economy, we need leaders who will capably and transparently oversee programs and put regulatory responsibility ahead of politics. This is essential to restoring faith and trust in state government, the integrity of Vermont’s regulatory system and the value of this economic development program.”

###

name: Diane Bedwell
subject: Bryant's ads → our grassroots response
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
They're on TV, we're on the ground doing the tough work to win this November. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTIwNDMwMAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTMyNTkzS0&mt=1&rt=0)
| Dear Friend, When I saw that Republican Bill Bryant was already up on TV, starting his ad blitz, I almost dropped my coffee. No doubt about it, we're in for a tough fight. As Governor Inslee's field director, I wanted to give you a sense of how we're responding. Because we're building this campaign the best way progressives in Washington state know how: From the ground up. Today, we have six field organizers across Washington, empowering volunteers, mobilizing voters, and building support in core communities. These organizers are the key to our campaign's strategy, and I'm so impressed by their passion and work ethic -- chances are you'll meet one of them at the door or on the phone soon! You and I both know that building a grassroots campaign takes resources. Just like grassroots organizing takes people power, so does grassroots fundraising. [**That 's why we've set a goal of 2,000 grassroots donations to start our field program off right -- can I count on you to be a part of this effort?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=18&ms=OTIwNDMwMAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTMyNTkzS0&mt=1&rt=0) Our opponent is hoping to win this election with an ad blitz. We're building our campaign from the grassroots on up. Our opponent is counting on the Koch brothers-funded Republican Governors Association to help him win this election. We're mobilizing communities to have their voices heard. As governor, Jay Inslee has fought to level the playing field and tackle problems with honest solutions. As a candidate, Jay Inslee is passionate about waging a people-powered campaign. That's why I'm so proud to be part of his team. subject: We've got some new info, and I want you to see it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
This race is closer than ever | Friend --
Ever since Donald Trump became the presumptive Republican nominee, you and I have been having a consistent conversation about what his candidacy means, how his campaign does its work, and what it's actually going to take to beat him.

So whenever we can share a new piece of evidence about where this race actually is, I want you to see it.

These are public polls, so we should take them with a big grain of salt. But if you hear people saying that Hillary can't possibly lose this race, make sure they see these numbers from yesterday morning:

[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjU5ODcwMzES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMTA5NTEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)
I’m not an alarmist, and you shouldn't be either. That’s not how you win elections.

**[But this election is going to be close. We really could lose, and these numbers are one of many reasons we should understand those basic facts to be true. Will you chip in $1 right now to show you're committed to winning?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjU5ODcwMzES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMTA5NTEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

After all, Donald Trump is running to win. He said so from the start.

Meanwhile, he’s about to announce his running mate, who will help him consolidate the Republican base -- that’s a poll bounce. He’s about to unleash a reality television circus of a national convention -- that’s another bounce. He’s raising big money fast. He’s hiring more and more people for his team -- and it seems that he's even started listening to his advisors from time to time.

**So don't accept for a second that this race is anything less than a sprint. Chip in $1 to get your free sticker and show that you're standing with Hillary, and let's go win this thing:**

[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjU5ODcwMzES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMTA5NTEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks -- and let's get back to work,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America
